false_negatives: Common benchmark pass-count regressions are missed if false counts remain zero.
Native-only or external-enhanced-only regressions can be missed because counts are not mode-scoped.
External provider degradation is documented but not detected as a structured post-edit regression.
false_positives: Content-token scanning marks many reports/docs as threshold_or_gate_changes when they merely mention production_gating_eligible or threshold.
Generated report changes are included in protected_changes when they contain protected tokens, producing noisy preflight/post-edit output.

issues_found.details: The baseline has common_native_passed/total and common_external_passed/total, but _baseline_regressions only checks false_clean_count, false_security_count, false_incomplete_count, and production_gating_eligible. A drop from 41/46 to a lower pass count can be missed if false counts stay at zero.
issues_found.impact: The harness should not yet block future coding-agent PRs because it can miss the primary protected common-suite regression it is meant to guard.
issues_found.title: Common benchmark pass totals are not compared against the protected baseline.

issues_found.details: find_key returns the first matching false-count key found in a nested report. For reports with modes.native and modes.external-enhanced, the selected count depends on JSON traversal order. Post-edit validation reports only one aggregate false_clean/security/incomplete value and does not verify both modes or either mode's pass total.
issues_found.impact: External-enhanced regressions can be hidden by native values, and vice versa.
issues_found.title: Native and external-enhanced mode results are not validated separately.

issues_found.details: It combines filename globs with content tokens, which correctly catches production_gating_eligible but also marks many generated reports and docs as threshold/gate changes. It does not explicitly protect common threshold/config locations beyond config/configs and filename matches.
issues_found.impact: High false positives reduce signal in dirty worktrees, while some threshold constants in ordinary source filenames may still be missed.
issues_found.title: Threshold/gate detection is useful but noisy and incomplete.

issues_found.details: workspace_audit sets safe_to_continue false when protected files changed, but status remains warn unless there are deleted tracked files. This is acceptable for advisory use, but too weak for a blocking harness mode.
issues_found.impact: Future agents may treat a protected-file warning as non-blocking unless they also inspect safe_to_continue.
issues_found.title: Protected changes are warnings even when safe_to_continue is false.

issues_found.details: The classifier checks critical first, so tests/fixtures is protected, but ordinary tests under tests/stresslab and decision-policy regression tests fall to low unless separately matched by another critical pattern. This is acceptable for general tests, but benchmark-integrity tests deserve a higher risk class.
issues_found.impact: Benchmark test changes can be under-emphasized in post-edit reports.
issues_found.title: Risk classification treats all tests as low risk before critical matching can distinguish benchmark tests.

issues_found.details: Tests are meaningful and fast, but they do not assert native/external-enhanced pass-total regression detection or degraded provider reporting because that logic is not implemented yet.
issues_found.impact: The current guardrail gap can regress silently.
issues_found.title: Tests cover core checks but not mode-separated benchmark regression parsing.

missing_guardrails: Compare native and external-enhanced common benchmark pass totals against protected_baseline.json.
Report false_clean_count, false_security_count, and false_incomplete_count per mode rather than via first-key recursive lookup.
Represent external-enhanced provider degradation from benchmark or launch reports explicitly in post-edit output.
Add stricter blocking semantics for protected changes under --strict or for future CI usage.
overengineering_concerns: The harness is not over-engineered: it is standard-library only, small, and audit-oriented.
The failure-mode registry is broad but still project-specific and acceptable as reference documentation.
recommended_next_smallest_improvement: Add mode-aware common benchmark parsing in harness_post_edit_validation.py and tests that fail on native or external-enhanced pass-count regressions below 41/46.

review_answers.checks_common_and_false_counts_and_gatin: partially; false counts and gating yes, common pass totals no
review_answers.degraded_external_providers_honest: documented in AGENTS/registry, not validated in reports
review_answers.json_markdown_reports_useful: yes, but noisy in dirty worktrees
